title: Aaron Allston

{{Quote|I read every Star Wars technical manual I could get my hands on, plus Stackpole's novels, Zahn's novels, other novels in which Wedge Antilles and Rogue Squadron make appearances, comic books, and several of West End's Star Wars game supplements. I watched the movie trilogy repeatedly. I played the X-Wing computer game. I bought eight of the Action Fleet toys and used them for measurements and estimations of their performance in atmosphere. I read books on aircraft carrier life and pilot survival.|Aaron Allston, about the research he did before writing|<ref name="Interview">{{WebCite|url=http://www.echostation.com/echo3/allston.htm|text=Interview with Aaron Allston|author=Jim Fisher|work=echostation.com|archivedate=20160412225900|nolive=1}}</ref>}}
'''Aaron Allston''' (d. [[February 27]], [[2014]]) was the author of thirteen ''Star Wars'' novels, and several short stories. His repertoire included the [[Wraith Squadron|Wraith Squadron Trilogy]], ''[[X-Wing: Starfighters of Adumar]]'', the [[Enemy Lines duology|''Enemy Lines'' duology]], three of the nine-book ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' series, followed by three of the nine-book [[Star Wars: Fate of the Jedi|''Fate of the Jedi'']] series, and returned to penning the adventures of the Wraith Squadron with ''[[X-Wing: Mercy Kill]]''. He also wrote the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] short stories ''[[The Clone Wars: The Pengalan Tradeoff]]'' and ''[[League of Spies]]''. He passed away on [[February 27]], [[2014]].
